concept.conceptName: Glucose [Moles/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration
Glucose [Mass/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration

en.name: Urinary glucose
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Urinary glucose is the concentration of glucose in a urine specimen — typically a spot (random) urine sample. It is commonly assessed by urine dipstick and is elevated when blood glucose exceeds the renal threshold for glucose reabsorption (~10 mmol/L in adults) [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser or a urine test strip. Results are reported as a substance concentration in mmol/L (SI unit) or as a mass concentration in mg/dL [2]. ## Included Concepts The set lists four LOINC "Lab Test" concepts measuring the glucose concentration in a spot urine specimen: - *3005570 / 15076-3 — Glucose [Moles/volume] in Urine (LOINC)*: spot (random) urine sample, SI form. Most widely used LOINC code. - *3020399 / 2350-7 — Glucose [Mass/volume] in Urine (LOINC)*: spot urine sample, mass concentration form. - *44786994 / 74351-8 — Glucose [Moles/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration (LOINC)*: used when the source does not specify whether the sample is spot or timed. - *3035214 / 35662-6 — Glucose [Mass/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mmol/L`** (concept 8753 — millimole per litre) for `[Moles/volume]` codes and **`mg/dL`** (concept 8840) for `[Mass/volume]` codes. Conversion: `1 mg/dL ≈ 0.0555 mmol/L`. ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concepts that exist for urinary glucose are deliberately not part of this set, grouped by reason for exclusion. ### Timed urine collections — different specimen (timed vs. spot) - *3007034 / 21305-8 — Glucose [Mass/volume] in 24 hour Urine* - *3035381 / 25916-8 — Glucose [Moles/volume] in 24 hour Urine* - *44816585 / 74687-5 — Glucose [Moles/volume] in 12 hour Urine* ### Excretion rates — different measurand (amount per unit time, not a concentration) - *3000361 / 15077-1 — Glucose [Moles/time] in 24 hour Urine* - *3005478 / 2351-5 — Glucose [Mass/time] in 24 hour Urine* - *3023544 / 18227-9 — Glucose [Mass/time] in 6 hour Urine* - *3005943 / 21306-6 — Glucose [Mass/time] in 8 hour Urine* - *3025893 / 21307-4 — Glucose [Mass/time] in 10 hour Urine* - *44816584 / 74686-7 — Glucose [Moles/time] in 12 hour Urine* - *40762090 / 58997-8 — Glucose [Mass/time] in 18 hour Urine* ### Ratios — different measurand (ratio to another analyte) - *3045427 / 33352-6 — Glucose/Creatinine [Mass Ratio] in Urine* - *3045505 / 34312-9 — Glucose/Creatinine [Molar ratio] in Urine* - *3040910 / 40488-9 — Glucose/Creatinine [Ratio] in Urine* - *40760123 / 57004-4 — Glucose/Creatinine [Mass Ratio] in 24 hour Urine* - *1989592 / 99602-5 — Glucose/Creatinine [Molar ratio] in 24 hour Urine* ### Qualitative — different measurand (presence, not a concentration) - *3020650 / 2349-9 — Glucose [Presence] in Urine* - *3007308 / 32174-5 — Glucose [Presence] in 24 hour Urine* ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "urinary glucose", "urine glucose", "spot urine glucose", "glycosuria" without further specification: `3005570 / 15076-3 — Glucose [Moles/volume] in Urine`. By far the most common LOINC code. - **Source reports a 24-hour (or other timed) urine collection**: do **not** map to this concept set. Timed urinary glucose is out of scope. - **Source reports a qualitative "glucose present" / dipstick result without a numeric value**: do **not** map. Qualitative glucose results are out of scope. - **Do not confuse with**: glucose in blood (separate concept set, "Glucose in blood"), urinary glucose excretion rate (mmol/24h). ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 15076-3 — Glucose [Moles/volume] in Urine. Available: https://loinc.org/15076-3/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
